title: juniperus sabina var. arenaria
intro: Juniperus sabina var. arenaria, also known as the Rocky Mountain juniper or Sabina, is a small evergreen shrub native to the high-altitude mountainous regions of the western United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic and to help reduce inflammation and improve circulation. The health benefits of Juniperus sabina var. arenaria include its potential to aid in the treatment of urinary tract infections, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic. Juniper berries from the plant can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being dried and used in herbal remedies, with the berries being considered particularly effective for their medicinal properties.
